example_posting.title: Context Engineer
example_posting.snippet: We are seeking a Context Engineer to design and optimize how large language models utilize their context windows. You'll work on novel techniques for efficient token usage, context compression, and strategic information placement to maximize model utility and cost-effectiveness.

core_responsibilities: Design and optimize context window strategies for LLMs
Develop techniques for efficient token usage and context compression
Evaluate and benchmark context approaches against baselines
Collaborate with alignment teams on context robustness
Document best practices and create tools for internal teams
typical_background: Strong Python and data manipulation skills
Experience with LLM APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic, Hugging Face)
Familiarity with prompt engineering and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG)
Understanding of transformer architectures and tokenization
notes: Often conflated with Prompt Engineer but represents a distinct specialization. Context Engineers focus on systemic/structural optimization of context usage, while Prompt Engineers craft individual prompts. Role emerged 2025 as context windows grew from 4k to 200k+ tokens. Growing fastest in frontier AI labs and enterprise AI applications.
pipeline_readiness_notes: Strong signal across multiple companies. Comparable to emerging ML roles; ready for task statement enrichment and scoring. Recommend assigning Job Zone 4 (advanced degree preferred but not required).

example_posting.title: Forward Deploy Engineer
example_posting.snippet: Forward Deploy Engineers embed with our most important customers to deploy AI systems at scale. You will architect solutions, troubleshoot production issues, develop integration patterns, and gather customer insights that inform product development. This role bridges customer needs and engineering.

core_responsibilities: Deploy and manage AI/ML systems at customer sites
Troubleshoot production issues and optimize performance
Develop custom integrations and solutions for enterprise customers
Gather product feedback and communicate customer needs to engineering
Write technical documentation and knowledge base articles
Mentor and support customer teams on AI best practices
typical_background: Knowledge of cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, Azure)
Familiarity with containerization and deployment pipelines
notes: Hybrid technical and customer-facing role that emerged 2026. Similar to Solutions Architect but focused on AI/ML stacks. Heavily concentrated in frontier AI labs and enterprise AI consulting. Represents growing need for customer deployment expertise. Sometimes titled 'Field AI Engineer' or 'Solutions Engineer - AI'.
pipeline_readiness_notes: Strong adoption by multiple companies; multi-company signal validates role legitimacy. Ready for pipeline. May map to existing Solutions Architect family in O*NET but with AI-specific variants.

example_posting.title: Design Engineer (AI)
example_posting.snippet: We're looking for Design Engineers to build AI-powered features that transform how creators work. This role sits at the intersection of design and engineering: you'll implement interactive prototypes, optimize AI models for responsive UX, and collaborate closely with designers and ML engineers.

core_responsibilities: Optimize model inference for sub-100ms latency
Collaborate with designers on AI interaction patterns
Build tools and frameworks for AI feature integration
Iterate rapidly on AI UX based on user feedback
typical_background: Frontend or full-stack software engineering background
Experience with UI frameworks and visualization
Familiarity with ML/AI concepts (not deep expertise required)
Strong design sensibility and UX understanding
notes: Blurs traditional design and engineering boundaries. Emerged as AI-native products required engineers comfortable with both UX and model integration. Title variance is high ('AI UI Engineer', 'ML Product Engineer', 'Interactive AI Engineer'). Concentrated in AI-native design/dev tools and consumer AI apps. Early stage; monitoring for sustained adoption.
pipeline_readiness_notes: Emerging signal but concentrated in design/tools vertical. Recommend monitoring for 1–2 more months to validate broader adoption before enrichment. May merge with 'Interaction Designer' role or diverge into separate specialization.
